src: καὶ ἦν ἡ παραβολὴ θαυμαστὴ πρὸ τῆς ἑρμηνείας"
ana: καὶ ἦν ἡ παραβολὴ θαυμαστὴ πρὸ τῆς ἑρμηνείας.
lit: And the parable was wonderful before the interpretation.
sm: And the parable was admirable before the interpretation.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.svo: Subject: ἡ παραβολή. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: θαυμαστή. Prepositional phrase: πρὸ τῆς ἑρμηνείας.
syn.rhet: Concludes the preceding comparison between parable and interpretation.

src: τοῦτ᾽ ἔστιν ὁ λαὸς ἦν τίμιος πρὸ τοῦ τὴν ἐκκλησίαν ἀνασταθῆναι,
lit: That is, the people was precious before the church was raised up.
sm: That is, the people was precious before the church was raised up.

syn.sub: πρὸ τοῦ + articular infinitive introduces the prior-time comparison.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ λαός. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος. The infinitival phrase τὴν ἐκκλησίαν ἀνασταθῆναι is governed by πρὸ τοῦ.
syn.rhet: Defines precedence of the people over the church in salvation-history.

src: καὶ ὃ νόμος θαυμαστὸς πρὸ τοῦ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον φωτισθῆναι.
ana: καὶ ὁ νόμος θαυμαστὸς πρὸ τοῦ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον φωτισθῆναι.
lit: And the law was wonderful before the gospel was enlightened.
sm: And the law was admirable before the gospel was made manifest.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ. The phrase πρὸ τοῦ + articular infinitive is retained as transmitted.

syn.sub: πρὸ τοῦ + infinitive introduces another prior-time comparison.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ νόμος. Verb: implied ἦν. Predicate adjective: θαυμαστός. Temporal phrase: πρὸ τοῦ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον φωτισθῆναι.
syn.rhet: Balances line 282 with a law/gospel contrast.

src: ὁπότε δὲ ἡ ἐκκλησία ἀνέστη
lit: Then when the church arose
sm: Then when the church arose,

syn.sub: ὁπότε introduces the temporal protasis that extends through line 285.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ ἐκκλησία. Verb: ἀνέστη.
syn.rhet: Signals the transition from the era of type to the era of fulfillment.

src: καὶ τὸ εὐαγγέλιον προέστη,
lit: and the gospel stood forth,
sm: and the gospel came forward,

syn.mc: Coordinate member of the temporal protasis begun in line 284.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ εὐαγγέλιον. Verb: προέστη.
syn.rhet: Church and gospel rise together as the decisive new reality.

src: ὁ τύπος ἐκενώθη παραδοὺς τῇ ἀληθείᾳ τὴν δύναμιν,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: The type was emptied, having handed over its power to the truth.
sm: The type was emptied, handing over its power to the truth.

syn.sub: The participle παραδοὺς explains the manner/result of ἐκενώθη.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Verb: ἐκενώθη. Participle: παραδοὺς. Dative recipient: τῇ ἀληθείᾳ. Object of participle: τὴν δύναμιν.
syn.rhet: Type yields its force to truth.

src: καὶ ὁ νόμος ἐπληρώθη παραδοὺς τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ τὴν δύναμιν.
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: And the law was fulfilled, having handed over its power to the gospel.
sm: And the law was fulfilled, handing over its power to the gospel.

syn.sub: The participle parallels παραδοὺς in line 286.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ νόμος. Verb: ἐπληρώθη. Participle: παραδοὺς. Dative recipient: τῷ εὐαγγελίῳ. Object: τὴν δύναμιν.
syn.ptc: παραδοὺς is the law's yielding to the gospel.
syn.rhet: Law and type are symmetrically subordinated to their fulfillments.

src: ὃν τρόπον ὁ τύπος κενοῦται τῷ φύσει ἀληθεῖ τὴν εἰκόνα παραδούς,
tok: aor act ptcp nom sg m
lit: In the manner in which the type is emptied, having handed over the image to what is true by nature,
sm: Just as the type is emptied, handing over the image to what is true by nature,

syn.sub: ὃν τρόπον introduces the comparison; the participle παραδούς qualifies ὁ τύπος.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Verb: κενοῦται. Dative recipient: τῷ φύσει ἀληθεῖ. Object: τὴν εἰκόνα. Participle: παραδούς.
syn.ptc: παραδούς explains the type's self-emptying in the comparative model.
syn.rhet: The comparative frame restates the typological transfer in abstract terms.

src: καὶ ἡ παραβολὴ πληροῦται ὑπὸ τῆς ἑρμηνείας φωτισθεῖσα,
tok: aor pass ptcp nom sg f
lit: And the parable is fulfilled by the interpretation, having been illuminated.
sm: And the parable is fulfilled through interpretation once it has been illuminated.

syn.sub: The participle φωτισθεῖσα gives the condition under which the parable is fulfilled.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ παραβολή. Verb: πληροῦται. Agent phrase: ὑπὸ τῆς ἑρμηνείας. Participle: φωτισθεῖσα.
syn.ptc: φωτισθεῖσα is circumstantial and anticipates the illuminating interpretation.
syn.rhet: Parable and interpretation are aligned in a reveal-and-fulfill pattern.

src: οὕτως δὴ καὶ ὃ νόμος ἐπληρώθη τοῦ εὐαγγελίου φωτισθέντος,
ana: οὕτως δὴ καὶ ὁ νόμος ἐπληρώθη τοῦ εὐαγγελίου φωτισθέντος,
tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg n
lit: Thus indeed also the law was fulfilled when the gospel had been illuminated.
sm: Thus indeed also the law was fulfilled when the gospel was illuminated.

syn.sub: τοῦ εὐαγγελίου φωτισθέντος is a genitive absolute expressing the temporal circumstance.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ νόμος. Verb: ἐπληρώθη. Genitive absolute: τοῦ εὐαγγελίου φωτισθέντος.
syn.rhet: The abstract comparison is reapplied to the law in a concise resumptive form.

src: καὶ ὃ λαὸς ἐκενώθη τῆς ἐκκλησίας ἀνασταθείσης"
ana: καὶ ὁ λαὸς ἐκενώθη τῆς ἐκκλησίας ἀνασταθείσης.
tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg f
lit: And the people was emptied when the church had been raised.
sm: And the people was emptied when the church had been raised.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: ὃ -> ὁ; trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.sub: τῆς ἐκκλησίας ἀνασταθείσης is a genitive absolute expressing prior circumstance.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ λαός. Verb: ἐκενώθη. Genitive absolute: τῆς ἐκκλησίας ἀνασταθείσης.
syn.rhet: The people is now said to be emptied in relation to the raised church.

src: καὶ ὃ τύπος ἐλύθη τοῦ κυρίου φανερωθῶντος,
ana: καὶ ὁ τύπος ἐλύθη τοῦ κυρίου φανερωθέντος,
tok: aor pass ptcp gen sg m
lit: And the type was dissolved when the Lord had been revealed.
sm: And the type was dissolved when the Lord was revealed.
emd: Followed change_log.txt: φανερωθῶντος -> φανερωθέντος; ὃ -> ὁ for the required article.

syn.sub: τοῦ κυρίου φανερωθέντος is a genitive absolute marking the revealing of the Lord as the condition of dissolution.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τύπος. Verb: ἐλύθη. Genitive absolute: τοῦ κυρίου φανερωθέντος.
syn.rhet: The type is explicitly undone by revelation.

src: καὶ σήμερον γέγονεν τά ποτε τίμια ἄτιμα
lit: And today the things once precious have become dishonored.
sm: And today what was once precious has become dishonored.

syn.sub: No subordinate clause; line 296 explains the basis of the change.
syn.svo: Subject: τά ποτε τίμια. Verb: γέγονεν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμα.
syn.rhet: The temporal marker σήμερον sharpens the reversal.

src: τῶν φύσει τιμίων φανερωθέντων.
tok: aor pass ptcp gen pl n
lit: When the things precious by nature had been revealed.
sm: when the things precious by nature had been revealed.

syn.svo: Genitive absolute: τῶν φύσει τιμίων φανερωθέντων.
syn.ptc: φανερωθέντων is the aorist passive participle governing the whole clause.
syn.rhet: The revelation of true value causes the reversal.

src: Ἦν γάρ ποτε τίμιος ἡ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγή,
ana: Ἦν γάρ ποτε τιμία ἡ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγή,
lit: For once the slaughter of the sheep was precious,
sm: For once the slaughter of the sheep was precious,
emd: Followed change_log.txt: τίμιος ἡ -> τιμία ἡ.

syn.mc: Main explanatory clause introducing the antithetical series.
syn.svo: Subject: ἡ τοῦ προβάτου σφαγή. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: τιμία.
syn.rhet: Sets up the precious/dishonored reversal.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου ζωήν"
ana: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου ζωήν.
lit: But now dishonored because of the life of the Lord.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the Lord's life.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.svo: Implied subject: ἡ σφαγή. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμος. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου ζωήν.
syn.rhet: The first reversal is stated as a sharp now/but contrast.

src: τίμιος ὃ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος,
lit: Precious the death of the sheep,
sm: The death of the sheep was precious,
emd: Followed change_log.txt pattern of article correction: ὃ -> ὁ.

syn.mc: Nominal clause in the antithetical series.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ τοῦ προβάτου θάνατος. Implied verb: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος.
syn.rhet: Second member of the precious/dishonored series.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου σωτηρίαν"
ana: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου σωτηρίαν.
lit: But now dishonored because of the salvation of the Lord.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the Lord's salvation.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.svo: Implied subject: ὁ θάνατος. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμος. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν τοῦ κυρίου σωτηρίαν.
syn.rhet: The second reversal reassigns value from death to salvation.

src: τίμιον τὸ τοῦ προβάτου αἷμα,
lit: Precious the blood of the sheep,
sm: The blood of the sheep was precious,

syn.mc: Nominal clause in the antithetical series.
syn.svo: Subject: τὸ τοῦ προβάτου αἷμα. Implied verb: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: τίμιον.
syn.rhet: Third member of the series shifts from death to blood.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμον διὰ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου πνεῦμα"
ana: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμον διὰ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου πνεῦμα.
lit: But now dishonored because of the spirit of the Lord.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the Lord's spirit.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.svo: Implied subject: τὸ αἷμα. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμον. Causal phrase: διὰ τὸ τοῦ κυρίου πνεῦμα.
syn.rhet: The third reversal replaces blood with spirit.

src: τίμιος ἄφωνος ἀμνός,
lit: Precious [the] voiceless lamb,
sm: The voiceless lamb was precious,

syn.mc: Nominal clause in the antithetical series.
syn.svo: Subject: ἄφωνος ἀμνός. Implied verb: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος.
syn.rhet: The sacrificial lamb is named directly before its reversal.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὸν ἄμωμον υἱόν"
ana: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὸν ἄμωμον υἱόν.
lit: But now dishonored because of the blameless Son.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the blameless Son.
emd: Trailing OCR quotation mark normalized to a period.

syn.svo: Implied subject: ὁ ἀμνός. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμος. Causal phrase: διὰ τὸν ἄμωμον υἱόν.
syn.rhet: The final reversal in this series moves from lamb to Son.

src: τίμιος ὁ κάτω ναός,
lit: Precious the lower temple,
sm: The lower temple was precious,

syn.mc: Nominal clause in the antithetical series.
syn.svo: Subject: ὁ κάτω ναός. Implied verb: ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος.
syn.rhet: The series expands from sacrificial figures to cultic space.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὸν ἄνω Χριστόν.
lit: But now dishonored because of the upper Christ.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the Christ above.

syn.svo: Implied subject: ὁ ναός. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμος. Causal phrase: διὰ τὸν ἄνω Χριστόν.
syn.rhet: The earthly sanctuary yields to the heavenly Christ.

src: ἦν τίμιος ἡ κάτω ᾿Ιερουσαλήμ,
lit: The lower Jerusalem was precious,
sm: The lower Jerusalem was precious,

syn.svo: Subject: ἡ κάτω Ἰερουσαλήμ. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος.
syn.rhet: The geographical/cultic contrast moves to Jerusalem.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν ἄνω ᾿Ιερουσαλήμ"
ana: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν ἄνω Ἰερουσαλήμ.
lit: But now dishonored because of the upper Jerusalem.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the upper Jerusalem.
emd: Removed stray spacing psili from ᾿Ιερουσαλήμ and normalized the trailing OCR quote to a period.

syn.svo: Implied subject: ἡ κάτω Ἰερουσαλήμ. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμος. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν ἄνω Ἰερουσαλήμ.
syn.rhet: Earthly Jerusalem is displaced by the heavenly one.

src: ἣν τίμιος ἡ στενὴ κληρονομία,
lit: The narrow inheritance was precious,
sm: The narrow inheritance was precious,

syn.svo: Subject: ἡ στενὴ κληρονομία. Verb: ἦν. Predicate adjective: τίμιος.
syn.rhet: The final contrast turns from city to inheritance.

src: νῦν δὲ ἄτιμος διὰ τὴν πλατεῖαν χάριν.
lit: But now dishonored because of the broad grace.
sm: But now it is dishonored because of the broad grace.

syn.mc: Main antithetical clause closing the paragraph.
syn.svo: Implied subject: ἡ στενὴ κληρονομία. Verb: implied ἐστίν. Predicate adjective: ἄτιμος. Causal phrase: διὰ τὴν πλατεῖαν χάριν.
syn.rhet: The series closes on a narrow/broad contrast: inheritance versus grace.
